gwas_traits: Rheumatoid arthritis (p=3e-9); Autoimmune hypothyroidism (p=3e-8); Psoriasis; SLE; T1D
note: 4-1BB/CD137. RECOVERED-KNOWN. Genome-wide RA + autoimmune-hypothyroidism GWAS at locus; blood eQTL (Lepik). Approved-stage drugs (urelumab/utomilumab) are AGONISTS for cancer -> pathway is pro-activation, so reduced function is immunosuppressive and plausibly protective in autoimmunity, but no coloc to confirm the RA risk-allele expression direction; direction unknown.

note: RECOVERED-KNOWN: type-I IFN receptor. ANIFROLUMAB (anti-IFNAR1 ANTAGONIST) is APPROVED for SLE -> IFNAR1 blockade (reduced function) is protective in IFN-driven autoimmunity. gwas_traits: Multiple sclerosis (p=2e-29, rs4939490); IBD; ankylosing spondylitis; Crohn; Graves; psoriasis
note: RECOVERED-KNOWN: ITOLIZUMAB (anti-CD6 INHIBITOR) is Phase-3 for GvHD/psoriasis/T1D. CD6 is a T-cell costimulatory molecule; inhibition is immunosuppressive -> reduced function protective in autoimmunity. Multiple genome-wide autoimmune hits, OT genetic 0.75 (IBD). Knockdown points the RIGHT WAY for an inhibitor.

gwas_traits: Hypothyroidism (p=3e-41, rs1921445); Autoimmune thyroid disease (OR 1.1, p=7e-19, rs57512118); MS; Hashimoto
note: RECOVERED-KNOWN: E3 ligase, established negative regulator (brake) of TCR signalling. Reduced CBLB -> MORE activation -> autoimmune-thyroid RISK (GWAS direction), OT genetic 0.81. For the ANTI-TUMOR brake-release goal, knockdown is the RIGHT way (immune-enhancing); the autoimmune-thyroid genetics is the on-target safety liability, NOT protection. Wrong way for an autoimmune-suppressing inhibitor.
